summary.intent: User was addressing review feedback on a merge request, including type annotations, API call ordering, JSON schema tightening, and formatting. After pushing the changes, tests that previously passed started failing.
summary.outcome: Identified that asyncio.get_event_loop() is deprecated in Python 3.10+ and causes RuntimeError when no event loop exists. Fixed by replacing asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete() with asyncio.run() in test_release_from_fbc.py.

repo: This codebase implements MR approval rules functionality that loads approver configuration from group config and sets GitLab MR approval rules
Has 35 pipeline tests and 74 validator tests

code.finding: Tests were using deprecated asyncio.get_event_loop() pattern which fails in Python 3.10+ when there's no current event loop. Must use asyncio.run() instead to create a new event loop.

workflow: Tests can pass in one environment but fail under make test due to stricter settings or different Python runtime behavior
asyncio.get_event_loop() is deprecated - asyncio.run() should be used to create and manage event loops in modern Python

friction: Tests passed initially (reported as 'all green') but failed when run via make test command
All 17 test failures had identical root cause (RuntimeError: no current event loop) masking the single underlying issue
